reviews.review_description:
I'm certain I left a review for this several years ago but can't see it now and the fact it's letting me write this must mean my review has been erased.

Great demo, graphics and sounds, just  shame the devs dumped it and never made a full game.
No longer launches on Oculus Go
Not bad at all for free it's very short and sweet with a twist in the story nice

With only the first fairly short chapter out its hard to review. The mechanics were fun and graphics pretty nice but beat it in probably 10 minutes(didn’t actually watch time). Soon as more comes I’ll rate a 5 if it keeps the same quality as this chapter.

I got stuck right inside the gate on my way out of the complex...  Didn't seem to be in any more mortal danger and scene would not advance.  Needs beta testing or alpha testing, or whatever the hell the field has come to call it these days
It is awesome￼￼

I enjoyed this short demo a lot, it's close to be perfect. I did not like that I am jumping from point to point and there is no walking happening. 
And I did not enjoyed the swordfight l, since the movement does not really use the capability of the gear controller. It's a stiff swing that is easy to miss.

You guys did an amazing job with that game and the optics, I enjoyed the graphics, although the mech seems a little bit to cute for the seriousness in this game. It does not line up with the fact that you can kill someone by headshot. 

Keep on your work!
I love this game. Keep it up
Proper story, not short and not 100% shooting. Works perfectly on Oculus Go. I think it's one of the best free apps, totally recommend!
This game is very immersive i liked it , but it could be 10 x better if its possible to freely move around instead of teleporting .
Definitely worth it for a free game. Just wish it was longer, but still worth it!

I’m about the other stuff rather than games on the OG, but this one is pretty fun. 
I’ve enjoyed it and don’t mind playing it over again. 
If you’re not a die hard gamer, then you should try this one. (I will let my grandson play this one, but I know he’ll be complete in about 15 minutes).
Good concept but little game

The game was okay. It was a little clunky and not real smooth. The responsiveness from the controller to the actual action was lagged. The concept is good but ness further in depth development as far as story line goes. Not a huge fan of pointing and clicking to move from one spot to another. Hopefully the next version will be more interactive with the user.
Not a bad game ive yet to use the proper controler dont have one use bt ipega joypad works but not the extent of being able to point and click will review when i get the proper controle pad
Great game, it's quick but very well done. Please update with new  levels.

But you could put more things into the game 
But fun for the game

reviews.review_title: Change of pace from Black River
reviews.review_description: Another experience from Black River Studios, which means another genre tackled and another expectation subverted. With this title, the studio has largely abandoned the quiet, almost poetic psychological approach to story-telling, and made a stab at action-packed science fiction. Compared to Angest, their previous sci-fi excursion, this is less Arthur C. Clarke and more George Lucas. There’s still the usual calling cards found in the studio’s work: the point-and-click teleportation system, the lack of any real exposition, and the signature twist ending. But in all actuality, the studio has largely attempted to do something different with their VR prowess, and they should be commended for it. At this point, Black River Studios is becoming the Stanley Kubrick of VR game developers. Not that these are masterpieces (and considering that they’re free, that should no longer be an expectation), but just like the late great director treated each of his films as an opportunity to explore a new genre, so has the studio with their VR stories. Their weakness for me has always been in their seeming unwillingness to craft a story with the depth of a true 3-act structure, as was the case with Angest and Dead Body Falls. Here, that shortcoming is doubled down. If you thought those experiences felt rushed, you ain’t seen nothing yet. It’s pretty much over once it starts. But I’ve come to realize that for better or worse, it’s just who they are. The fact that they have successfully and consistently translated their philosophical visions to multiple genres while maintaining artistic integrity-AND not charging us the audience anything along the way-means they are at the forefront of VR storytelling and app development. I enthusiastically recommend this experience, like all their others, and look forward to their next journey. You should too.
Not bad for about 5 minutes but soon got bored with it
Game was good
